growth_records.medium: MMT-KDP (marine minimal Tris medium + KH2PO4) with laminarin as sole carbon source

up.method: reported medium substrate concentration; specific qS not derivable (no biomass/gDW basis reported)

growth_records.cite: Xu et al. Phosphate deprivation restricts bacterial degradation of the marine polysaccharide fucoidan. Nature Microbiology (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41564-025-02240-z. [PMC12872454]
growth_records.snippet: For laminarin, the growth rate remained relatively constant at 0.15 h-1 between 1 uM and 50 uM phosphate concentration (r = -0.76, P < 0.05).
growth_records.notes: Growth rate reported directly as 0.15 h-1 (roughly constant across 1-50 uM Pi on laminarin). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.15 = 4.62 h. Temperature = room temperature (r.t., value not given numerically). pH 7.8 from Tris buffer. Phosphate-limitation laminarin experiments used 0.05% (w/v) laminarin as sole C source; laminarin at 0.05% corresponds to ~14,911 uM glycan carbon per Methods. No qS/qP in mmol/gDW/h derivable (no biomass or gDW normalization reported).

growth_records.medium: MMT-KDP (marine minimal Tris medium + 50 uM KH2PO4) with Glossomastix fucoidan as sole carbon source

up.method: reported medium substrate concentration; specific qS not derivable

growth_records.cite: Xu et al. Phosphate deprivation restricts bacterial degradation of the marine polysaccharide fucoidan. Nature Microbiology (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41564-025-02240-z. [PMC12872454]
growth_records.snippet: With fucoidan, the growth rate was overall lower and decreased linearly from 0.13 h-1 at 50 uM to 0.06 h-1 at 1 uM phosphate concentration (r = 0.98, P < 0.001).
growth_records.notes: Growth rate reported directly as 0.13 h-1 at 50 uM phosphate on Glossomastix fucoidan. doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.13 = 5.33 h. Fucoidan 0.05% (w/v) = 5592.98 uM glycan carbon (Methods). Temperature = room temperature (numeric value not given). No mmol/gDW/h fluxes derivable.

growth_records.medium: MMT-KDP (marine minimal Tris medium + 1 uM KH2PO4) with Glossomastix fucoidan as sole carbon source

up.method: reported medium substrate concentration; specific qS not derivable

growth_records.cite: Xu et al. Phosphate deprivation restricts bacterial degradation of the marine polysaccharide fucoidan. Nature Microbiology (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41564-025-02240-z. [PMC12872454]
growth_records.snippet: decreased linearly from 0.13 h-1 at 50 uM to 0.06 h-1 at 1 uM phosphate ... at 1 uM phosphate, the mean growth rate was 2.98-fold slower with fucoidan than with laminarin.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate reported directly as 0.06 h-1 at 1 uM phosphate on fucoidan (phosphate deprivation). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.06 = 11.55 h. At 1 uM Pi, fucoidan mu is 2.98-fold below laminarin (0.15/2.98 approx 0.05, consistent with reported 0.06). Cell count at 1 uM Pi on fucoidan = (1 +/- 4) x10^6 cells/mL (linear model 1.88x10^6) vs laminarin (27 +/- 6) x10^6. No mmol/gDW/h fluxes derivable.
